# Decision Log (Hybrid)

## Purpose
Capture high-impact decisions, rationale, and delivery impacts across stage gates and sprints to
ensure transparency and accountability.

## Audience
Project Manager, Steering Committee, Delivery Leads, PMO.

## When to Use
Use for any decision that affects scope, schedule, budget, quality, or risk posture.

---

## 1. Decision Log Summary
| Project Name | [Project Name] |
| --- | --- |
| Project Manager | [Name] |
| Last Updated | [Date] |
| Total Decisions | [Number] |
| Pending Decisions | [Number] |
| Next Review Date | [Date] |

### Decision Status Summary
- **Approved:** [Number]
- **Pending:** [Number]
- **Rejected:** [Number]
- **Under Review:** [Number]

---

## 2. Decision Log Entries

### Decision ID: [PROJ-DEC-001]
**Decision Title:** [Brief, descriptive title]

**Dates**
- **Date raised:** [Date]
- **Date decided:** [Date]

**Status:** [Approved/Pending/Rejected/Under Review]
**Category:** [Technical/Business/Process/Resource/Scope/Schedule/Budget]
**Priority:** [High/Medium/Low]
**Impact Level:** [High/Medium/Low]

#### Decision Details
**Background / Context**
[Why this decision is required and the conditions that triggered it.]

**Decision Statement**
[Clear, concise statement of what was decided.]

**Options Considered**
1. **Option 1:** [Description]
   - **Pros:**
   - **Cons:**
   - **Cost:**
   - **Risk:**
2. **Option 2:** [Description]
   - **Pros:**
   - **Cons:**
   - **Cost:**
   - **Risk:**
3. **Option 3:** [Description]
   - **Pros:**
   - **Cons:**
   - **Cost:**
   - **Risk:**

**Rationale**
[Why this option was selected, including key trade-offs.]

#### Decision Authority
- **Decision maker:** [Name, title]
- **Decision body:** [Committee or forum]
- **Consulted:** [Names]
- **Informed:** [Names]

#### Impact Assessment
- **Scope impact:**
- **Schedule impact:**
- **Budget impact:**
- **Resource impact:**
- **Quality impact:**
- **Risk impact:**
- **Phase impact:** [Stage gate(s) affected]
- **Sprint impact:** [Sprint(s) affected]

#### Implementation
**Action Items**
| Action | Owner | Due Date | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |

**Communication Plan**
- **Who needs to know:**
- **Method:**
- **Date:**
- **Owner:**

**Success Criteria**
[How success will be measured after implementation.]

#### Review & Updates
**Review Date:** [Date]
**Related Decisions:** [IDs]
**Dependencies:** [List]

**Updates Log**
| Date | Update | Updated By | Reason |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |

---

## 3. Decision Categories
- **Technical:** Architecture, tools, platform choices.
- **Business:** Scope, requirements, business rules.
- **Process:** Methodology, governance, delivery approach.
- **Resource:** Team allocation, vendor changes.
- **Schedule:** Timeline adjustments, milestone changes.
- **Budget:** Funding changes, cost trade-offs.

## 4. Priority & Impact Definitions
- **High:** Blocks progress or changes baseline (>10% impact).
- **Medium:** Noticeable impact (2–10%).
- **Low:** Minor adjustments (<2%).

## 5. Decision Review Cadence
- **Weekly (PM):** Review pending decisions and update status.
- **Monthly (Steering):** Review major decisions and impacts.
- **Stage gate reviews:** Confirm decisions required for gate approval.

## 6. Decision Metrics
- Average time from raise to decision.
- % decisions made within target SLA.
- % decisions reversed or revisited.
- Total budget/schedule impact of decisions.

## 7. Communication Templates
### Decision Announcement Email
**Subject:** [Project Name] – Decision Made: [Decision Title]

**Summary:**
A decision has been made regarding [brief description].

**Decision:**
[Clear statement of what was decided]

**Rationale:**
[Key reasons and trade-offs]

**Impacts:**
- **Timeline:**
- **Budget:**
- **Scope:**
- **Resources:**

**Next Steps:**
[Actions and owners]

---

## Completion Checklist
- [ ] Decision statement and rationale documented.
- [ ] Impact assessment completed with phase/sprint impacts.
- [ ] Decision maker and consulted parties recorded.
- [ ] Implementation actions assigned and tracked.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Decision log reflects approved, traceable decisions.
- Stakeholders can identify impacts across phases and sprints.
- Decisions include owners, rationale, and follow-up actions.
